mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary policy supporting consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2 percent quarterly while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and higher consumer income, which directly supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.01 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 950 thousand units, the housing sector contraction signals broader demand weakness and reduced construction-related economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 55 percent of months where HOUST stays below 950 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant or rising federal funds rate indicates restrictive monetary conditions that compress equity valuations and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive forward returns when CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.3 percent monthly while FEDFUNDS rises by less than 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms robust economic expansion and rising corporate profit expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during early-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0 percent fails to predict positive 60-day returns when the yield curve is inverted for more than three months.
